data.title: DeFi Yields Lag Behind Traditional Treasury Returns
data.description: Aave V3's USDC deposit rate is currently around 2.7%, which is lower than the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield of 4.3%, according to Tiger Research. This indicates that decentralized finance (DeFi) yields no longer provide a distinct premium over traditional financial instruments. According to NS3.AI, Tiger Research also highlighted that real-world assets (RWA) and stablecoins have expanded into sectors worth hundreds of billions of dollars. This growth reflects a market transition from token-incentive models to products supported by external cash flows.

data.title: New Address Invests in Prediction Market on Iran-U.S. Peace Agreement
data.description: A newly established address has spent $52,000 on predictions regarding the Iran-U.S. peace agreement, according to Odaily. The address invested in the outcome that a permanent peace agreement would not be reached by May 15. Additionally, it made smaller investments predicting that the agreement would not be finalized by May 31. Previously, the address had only invested in predictions that a permanent peace agreement would not be achieved by April 22 and April 30.

data.title: Bank of England Chief Economist: Tightening Financial Conditions Reasonable Amid Inflation Risks from Iran Conflict
data.description: The Bank of England's Chief Economist, Huw Pill, has stated that tightening financial conditions appears to be a reasonable response to the inflation risks posed by the ongoing conflict in Iran. According to Jin10, Pill emphasized the potential impact of the war on inflation, suggesting that financial measures may be necessary to mitigate these risks.

data.title: Record US Stock Highs Mask Dangerous Valuations Second Only to Dot-Com Bubble, FT Warns
data.description:
Key Takeaways
The S&P 500's trailing P/E ratio stands at approximately 24 as of April 2026, well above the historical average of 16, per Financial Times reportingThe Shiller cyclically adjusted P/E ratio has risen above 37 -- the second-highest level in history, surpassed only by the dot-com bubble peakThe current rally is built on a combination of optimistic assumptions including AI-driven profit growth, declining inflation, lower interest rates, and manageable geopolitical risk -- any deviation could trigger outsized market shocksThe "valuation plus high expectations" combination leaves the market with an extremely limited margin for error
Record highs in the S&P 500 and Nasdaq are masking a set of structural vulnerabilities that leave US equity markets with little room for disappointment, the Financial Times reported on May 1, warning that current valuations have reached levels second only to the dot-com era in modern market history.
As of April 2026, the S&P 500's trailing price-to-earnings ratio stands at approximately 24 -- roughly 50% above the historical average of 16. More strikingly, the Shiller P/E ratio, which adjusts for cyclical earnings fluctuations over a 10-year period to provide a longer-term valuation benchmark, has climbed above 37. That reading places current equity valuations at their second-highest level ever recorded, exceeded only by the peak of the dot-com bubble in the late 1990s and early 2000s -- a period that preceded one of the most severe market crashes in US history.
A Market Built on Optimistic Assumptions
The FT report identifies the structural fragility underlying the record highs as stemming not just from elevated valuations but from the combination of high prices and the optimistic assumptions required to justify them. The current rally is predicated on a specific set of conditions holding simultaneously: AI-driven corporate profit growth continuing to accelerate, inflation declining toward the Fed's 2% target, interest rates moving lower, and geopolitical risks -- including the ongoing US-Iran conflict -- remaining contained and manageable.
The danger lies in the interdependence of these assumptions. Any single deviation -- a hawkish Fed pivot, an oil price spike that keeps inflation elevated, a geopolitical escalation, or a disappointment in AI-driven earnings growth -- could trigger a magnified market reaction precisely because current valuations leave so little buffer. When markets are priced for perfection, imperfect outcomes produce disproportionate corrections.
Crypto Market Implications
For Bitcoin and digital assets, the equity valuation warning carries direct relevance. The strong positive correlation between crypto and US equities that has characterized the current cycle means a significant equity market repricing -- triggered by any of the risk factors identified by the FT -- would likely pressure crypto markets simultaneously. Bitcoin's ongoing struggle to clear $79,000--$80,000 resistance, combined with thinning spot market liquidity and persistent short positioning in derivatives, leaves it particularly exposed to a risk-off equity shock if the optimistic assumptions underpinning record stock highs begin to unravel.

data.title: Brother Machi continues to bet on ETH, increasing his long positions to $23.29 million.
data.description: Hyperbot data shows that Brother Machi continues to bet on long positions, with ETH long positions increasing to $23.29 million and BTC long positions increasing to $14.53 million. Both are currently far from being liquidated.

data.description: Hyperbot data reveals that significant long positions have been taken in Ethereum (ETH) and Bitcoin (BTC). According to Odaily, the long position in ETH has increased to $23.29 million, while the BTC long position has risen to $14.53 million. Both positions are currently far from liquidation.

data.title: Tapp Exchange to Cease Operations by 2026
data.description:
Tapp Exchange has announced plans to gradually cease operations, with the protocol remaining fully operational until May 31, 2026. According to ChainCatcher, all user assets are secure and can be withdrawn at any time.

Since its launch in June 2025, Tapp has developed the first V4-style decentralized exchange (DEX) on Aptos. The platform introduced mechanisms such as ve(3,3) voting, emission allocation, bribery infrastructure, and governance tools, empowering veTAPP holders with liquidity decision-making authority.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Pentagon Signs AI Agreements with SpaceX, OpenAI, Google, and Nvidia
data.description: The U.S. Pentagon has announced the completion of agreements related to cyber artificial intelligence. According to Jin10, the agreements have been signed with SpaceX, OpenAI, Google, and Nvidia. These partnerships aim to enhance the Pentagon's capabilities in AI-driven cybersecurity measures.

data.title: Cboe Global Markets Implements Workforce Reductions and Policy Changes
data.description: Cboe Global Markets has announced a significant reduction in its global workforce, cutting 20% of its employees. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that the company is also revising its return-to-office policies and providing voluntary retirement packages to senior staff. These measures are part of Cboe's strategic efforts to streamline operations and adapt to evolving market conditions. The decision reflects broader trends in the financial industry as companies reassess their workforce strategies in response to changing economic landscapes.

data.title: UK Fuel Retailers Maintain Margins Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Fuel retailers in the United Kingdom largely maintained their profit margins during the initial impact of the conflict involving Iran, according to the UK's antitrust watchdog. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that despite the geopolitical tensions, there was no significant change in the pricing strategies of these retailers. The watchdog's observation comes amid concerns about potential price hikes due to the instability in the region. The report suggests that the retailers' decision to keep margins steady helped mitigate immediate economic disruptions for consumers.

data.title: Tokenisation to Surge 5,600% to $2 Trillion by 2028, Says Standard Chartered
data.description: Tokenisation is projected to grow by 5,600% to reach a $2 trillion market by 2028, according to Standard Chartered. The bank attributes this growth to the expansion of decentralised finance (DeFi) lending via stablecoins, which facilitate the migration of real-world assets like stocks and bonds onchain. Geoffrey Kendrick, global head of digital assets research at Standard Chartered, highlighted the resilience of the DeFi sector despite recent challenges, such as the $300 million exploit of KelpDAO. Kendrick argues that rapid stabilisation efforts and structural upgrades bolster the long-term prospects for DeFi banking and stablecoin liquidity, supporting the projected market growth.

data.title: Moderna's Stock Rises Over 10% Pre-Market as Q1 Revenue Exceeds Expectations
data.description: Moderna's stock experienced a significant increase of over 10% in pre-market trading. According to Jin10, the company's first-quarter revenue surpassed market expectations, contributing to the stock's upward movement.

data.title: ECB Official Mahrouf: Inflation Expectations Require Close Monitoring
data.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Mahrouf emphasized the importance of closely monitoring inflation expectations. According to Jin10, Mahrouf highlighted the need to watch for any signs of de-anchoring in these expectations, which could have significant implications for monetary policy.

data.title: ECB's Makhlouf: Wage-Driven Inflation Effects May Take Longer to Emerge
data.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Gabriel Makhlouf has indicated that due to the staggered nature of wage setting, the secondary inflation effects potentially arising from wage channels may take a longer time to manifest. According to Jin10, Makhlouf's comments highlight the complexities involved in predicting inflationary trends linked to wage adjustments.

data.title: ExxonMobil Shares Rise 1.8% Pre-Market as Q1 Profits Exceed Expectations
data.description: ExxonMobil's stock saw a 1.8% increase in pre-market trading following the announcement of its first-quarter profits surpassing expectations. According to Jin10, the oil giant's financial performance has been positively received by investors, contributing to the uptick in share price.

data.title: ExxonMobil and Chevron Reject White House's Call for Increased Production Amid Energy Crisis
data.description:
ExxonMobil and Chevron have declined the White House's request to increase oil production, maintaining their current strategies despite the U.S. government's efforts to address the most severe energy crisis in decades. According to Jin10, ExxonMobil CFO Neil Hansen stated that the company's strategy in the Permian Basin, a major U.S. oil and gas region, remains unchanged. Chevron CFO Eimear Bonner echoed this sentiment, noting that the crisis has not prompted any changes to their plans.

The ongoing conflict in Iran has significantly reduced oil output in the Gulf region, impacting refining operations in the Middle East and beyond, potentially exacerbating global inflation. In response, the U.S. government has released strategic petroleum reserves and urged the industry to increase drilling activities. However, these two American energy giants continue to adhere to their pre-crisis strategies.

Hansen remarked, "We don't see a need to accelerate because our production is already high and operating at full capacity. While we have considered the possibility of expanding production, there are indeed constraints." Bonner added, "We could increase production in the Permian, but that's not our strategy. Our focus is on increasing free cash flow, not production. You can't expect us to drastically change plans due to an eight-week supply disruption."

These statements were made as both companies reported their first-quarter earnings on Friday.

data.title: ExxonMobil and Chevron Resist Pressure to Increase Oil Production
data.description: ExxonMobil and Chevron have resisted pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump to increase oil production, according to Odaily. ExxonMobil stated that the closure of the Strait of Hormuz throughout the quarter is expected to result in a 3% decrease in global product solutions throughput in the second quarter compared to the fourth quarter of 2025.

data.title: ExxonMobil defies pressure from Trump and refuses to increase oil production.
data.description: According to the Financial Times, ExxonMobil (XOM.N) and Chevron (CVX.N) withstood pressure from US President Trump and refused to increase oil production. (Jinshi)

data.title: MENA Region Faces Potential Disruption Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: The ongoing conflict involving Iran may lead to increased demand for on-chain settlement in the MENA region, according to NS3.AI. A potential closure of the Strait of Hormuz poses a threat to approximately 20% of the global oil supply, highlighting the geopolitical risks in the area. Additionally, Saudi Arabia has suspended marine vessel documentation requirements for 30 days, further indicating regional instability. TOKEN2049 Dubai's decision to postpone its event to 2027 also reflects the uncertainty affecting the region.
